battery.target: 5 cold reset-to-seed rebuilds, all graded gates green
battery.status: PASSED — with TWO disclosed caveats (below). Neither is hidden, and neither is rounded away.
battery.coldness_proof_per_cycle: PG_VERSION mtime read from INSIDE the container and compared against the cycle-start fence (initdb provably re-ran) + data dir GONE (purged) + images remaining 0 + a FRESH autoverify.json timestamp

gate_evidence.no_junk_skills: all 132 distinct claimed skill names ENUMERATED AND READ; 3,474 user_skill rows / 540 members; 0 junk
gate_evidence.hero_roles: hero role titles present (D2 — Business Operations Analyst resolves WITH job_role_skills)

caveat_1_orchestration.what: The battery is NOT a single uncontested consecutive run. Two further runs were graded RED for BATTERY-INTEGRITY reasons caused by an ORCHESTRATION error, not a demo defect: the orchestrator ran two batteries CONCURRENTLY against the single demo host, and one purged the stack mid-measurement while the other was reading it. Cycle 5's no-junk-skills gate consequently went UNEXECUTED.
caveat_1_orchestration.rule: An unexecuted gate is a FINDING, not a pass (D17: absence must never read as success).
caveat_1_orchestration.honest_statement: No demo defect was observed in anything that WAS measured. The 5 greens are each individually evidenced. They were not produced by one clean consecutive sweep, and this milestone does not pretend otherwise — recording it as a clean 5/5 would be precisely the 'status artifact that outlives the thing it describes' pattern this release exists to kill.

caveat_2_graded_is_not_shipped.what: The code that GRADED is not the code that SHIPPED. Two rext commits landed AFTER the graded tag cue-to-cue-m219-r8, during harden/close.
caveat_2_graded_is_not_shipped.b5bf65b: the coverage-harness stale-report fix — HARNESS ONLY, not the demo runtime (it grades the demo, it is not in it)
caveat_2_graded_is_not_shipped.c6648d1: aiReadinessStep1Score double-rounded: it routed through a 0-100 intermediate (round(round(held/total*100) * 30/100)) while the platform's computeTier1 rounds ONCE. They disagree on 3 of the 14 reachable heldWeights (2.5 -> 11 vs 12 | 4.0 -> 19 vs 18 | 5.5 -> 26 vs 25). THIS IS A SEED-PATH CHANGE.
caveat_2_graded_is_not_shipped.rule: Per M218 D13, a seed-path change RESTARTS the battery count. So the shipped code is not the code the 5 greens graded.
caveat_2_graded_is_not_shipped.impact: Small and strictly corrective — a seeded Step-1 score off by one point for some members; it does NOT affect whether any section renders. The fix makes the seed AGREE with the platform where it previously disagreed.
caveat_2_graded_is_not_shipped.not_rounded_away: D-M219-15 honoured this same rule once already, re-running a 35-minute battery rather than argue a fix was 'behaviourally identical'. The rule does not bend for a small delta.

alignment.delta_vs_m218: 97.2% -> 100.0% — FIX-M219-bapi-org-eid (F-11) landed: the roster's real org_eid is wired through a 3-tier ladder (roster -> demo-org -> stub), so exactly one gene moves and the runner stays byte-identical.

red_gene_resolved.was: deliberately RED at M218 (the fake BAPI fabricated organization.public_metadata.eid)
red_gene_resolved.now: GREEN — and the gene is RETAINED in the DNA as a permanent fence

unmeasurable_surface_gate.was: expressrun exited rc=2 with NO score, and nothing treated that as a failure (the D17 absence-read-as-success class)
unmeasurable_surface_gate.now: alignctl exit codes SPLIT: 3 = UNMEASURABLE vs 2 = REGRESSED, with a banner that cannot be mistaken for a pass. Verified live.

alignment.provenance: Recorded from the S3 build/verify run (progress.md S3). NOT re-measured at close — the close is documentation + bookkeeping only.

fences.graded_by: MUTATION — each fence was graded by surgically re-introducing the defect it claims to catch into HEAD's production code, rather than by re-running it green. The release's rule: a fence which passes against both the pre- and post-fix code is theatre.
fences.self_catch: The poisoned-pool fence's FIRST cut PASSED against the broken code (it did not model name attrition) — theatre, inside its own fix. Strengthened until it went RED pre-fix. See D-M219-18.

added_this_milestone.name: next-web-aireadiness-flag-gate
added_this_milestone.why: The MEMBER surface never mounts on a demo: a demo bakes no PostHog, so useFeatureFlagEnabled() resolves `undefined` FOREVER and the code demands `=== true`. No env/config/compose seam exists (the flag's value is not an env var), so it takes the sanctioned hatch. Behaviour-identical wherever PostHog IS configured.

withdrawn.name: the planned CycleID == nil patch
withdrawn.why: WITHDRAWN — the blocker DOES NOT EXIST. The CURRENT frontend passes ?cycle=; the original note was made against the LEGACY page, whose hook takes no cycle param. A non-patch fix was available (point the demo at the current surface), which is the correct order of preference per demopatch-spec.md §1. See D-M219-2.

bugs_fixed.close_breakdown: progress.md's § Battery claimed the battery ran 'at final code' — true when written, false by the close (two commits landed after the graded tag, one of them seed-path). Corrected in place and routed to M221. The milestone's own status artifact, D17 shape, for the second time.

refuted_premises.note: TWO of the milestone's own plan premises were REFUTED by measurement — which is the defining fact of M219, and the reason the planned demo-patch was never built.
refuted_premises.F-2: The CycleID == nil blocker DOES NOT EXIST. The CURRENT frontend passes ?cycle=; the original observation was made against the LEGACY page. => the planned demo-patch was WITHDRAWN.
refuted_premises.F-7: M217's loadmembers patch is NOT dead — it self-heals. And 'the live recompute never completes' is FALSE: 2.09 s, measured (reported, never gated, per D-DESIGN-1).

to_M220: The academy POISONS the demo session — item (i), SEVERITY RAISED to demo-BREAKING (cookies scope by HOST not PORT; a presenter who clicks the academy link is LOGGED OUT of the demo into ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS, and every employee coverage sweep aborts)
studio-desk :19000 -> 302 -> :13000/login — item (j), added at close (clicking 'Anthropos Studio' EJECTS the presenter from the demo)
to_M221: GUARD-M221-host-isolation — a host lock (or a per-cycle stack N) so two agents can NEVER run cycles against one demo host. A PREREQUISITE for M221's own gate, which is itself a multi-cycle battery on that same singleton host.
FIX-M221-reap-native-academy — down --purge does not reap the host-native academy; the OLD process keeps answering :13077 across cycles while the launcher log says it 'DIED'
REPROVE-M221-battery-at-final-code — the shipped code is NOT the code the 5 greens graded (c6648d1, seed-path, landed post-r8)
carry_forward.note: All 5 are Fate-3 and BOTH receiving overview.md files were EDITED (verified). Zero escape-hatch deferrals — nothing leaves v2.3. The two M220 items ship with HONEST FENCES that deliberately report RED until M220 lands: a half-fix that reports green is worse than no fix.

fates.fate_1_done: items 1-9 + Delivers + F-13 + the item-8 seeder gaps (declared in-scope by overview.md § Out)
fates.fate_2_confirmed_covered: the manager page's SPEED — declared Out, owned by M218 (gate met). M219 REPORTS it (2.09 s cold) per D-DESIGN-1.
fates.dropped: the planned CycleID == nil demo-patch (premise refuted, D-M219-2) + the loadmembers re-pin (premise refuted, F-7 — the patch self-heals)

d17_tally.hazard: D17 — a status artifact that outlives the thing it describes, and is then read as evidence. Named in M218.
d17_tally.sharpest: run-coverage.sh printed 'coverage report written to ...' UNCONDITIONALLY and re-read whatever coverage-report.json was on disk — so a spec that threw before assembling a report presented the PREVIOUS run's numbers, 'GATE: MET' and all, exiting 0. It nearly graded an M219 rebuild on hours-old data from the OLD, BROKEN stack.
d17_tally.the_test_was_the_bug: 17 existing seeder tests asserted the junk-fallback AS THE CONTRACT ('unmatched role must fall back to flat pool'; an expected value literally containing K-JUNK-1). They were not missing the bug — they were GUARDING it. Any correct fix was guaranteed to fail them. All inverted.
d17_tally.found_by_the_tooling_in_itself: the coverage harness, the seeder's own fence (its first cut passed against the broken code), and the academy launcher (whose test FIXTURES had encoded the broken behaviour — the suite was GREEN for four releases while the academy 502'd)
generalized_lessons: 'the role classifies' != 'the pool is big enough'
an errored SQL query is not 'zero rows'
an unexecuted gate is a FINDING, not a pass

inherited_from_m218_landed.expressrun_unmeasurable: LANDED — exit 3 (UNMEASURABLE) is now distinct from exit 2 (REGRESSED) and fails loud
inherited_from_m218_landed.freshness_gate_skips: LANDED — the silent skip now SPEAKS (it printed nothing). Plus three unit tests that deferred to a 'live-verify gate' THAT DOES NOT EXIST now report themselves as coverage holes, not passes.

known_issues.what: dev-stack test suite fails on this box
known_issues.status: environmental — needs a live Postgres on :15432; this box's .agentspace/secrets is also incomplete. Identical at v2.2, M217, and M218.
known_issues.m219_effect: none — M219 does not touch dev-stack/

notes: The defining fact of M219 is that TWO of its own plan premises were REFUTED by measurement, and the planned demo-patch was WITHDRAWN rather than built. The page was not blank for the reason the plan said: all 3 demo pointers targeted an UNLINKED ORPHAN (no nav, no tab, no redirect), and the employee surface has no route of its own at all (it is embedded in /home) — which is WHY route-crawling never found it. The junk-skill defect closed on arithmetic: want=28, role pool=10, curated 'data' supplied only 25 usable => exactly 3 junk tokens; Ben was clean only because his want (16) fit his 30-name pool, and THAT ASYMMETRY WAS THE PROOF the defect was pool SIZE, not pool resolution. The flat tier is now DELETED (exhausted => FEWER skills, never padded ones). Both the manager's empty interview-findings blocks and the 120-char floor that passed over them are gone (the empty headings measured ~120-200 chars, which is exactly why 120 passed).
